message:
perf(vdbe): early-return ensure_storage_cursor_row_layout when layout is already good

`ensure_storage_cursor_row_layout` was always falling through to the
buffer-resize / payload-refetch path even when the cursor's row decode
table was already populated and the payload buffer already covered
the requested span. The work that follows (computing
`required_bytes`, possibly reallocating, possibly re-reading the
payload from the pager, re-running the decode) is the right path
when something actually changed — but it's a hot-loop call from
the storage cursor's per-row inner loop, so the no-op case is the
common case and was paying the full cost of the path on every row.

Add a fast-path early return at the top of the function:

```rust
if !refreshed
    && !cursor.row_decode.is_empty()
    && cursor.payload_buf.len() >= min_payload_bytes
{
    return Ok(DecodeCacheRefreshState {
        refreshed: false,
        eager_values_ready: false,
    });
}
```

The guard is precisely the conjunction of "we have not just refreshed
the layout" + "the row decode table already has entries" + "the
payload buffer is at least as large as the minimum the caller asked
for". Under those conditions the layout is by definition still valid
(no schema-cookie change since the last refresh, no buffer truncation,
no row-decode reset) and the function has nothing to do.

`refreshed = false` and `eager_values_ready = false` mirror the
return values of the pre-existing slow path when no eager-values
work was triggered, so callers that branch on either flag see
identical behavior to the no-op slow path traversal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
